name: ivre

documentation.readme: IVRE (Instrument de veille sur les réseaux extérieurs) or DRUNK (Dynamic Recon of UNKnown networks) is a network recon framework, including tools for passive and active recon. IVRE can use data from:
usage_sections: (in English): Know the networks, get DRUNK!

arguments.name: -side
arguments.description: this server-side knob does not need to change.

examples: IVRE contributors. *IVRE, a network recon framework*.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `IVRE ships an [MCP]($KFIOSA_TARGET_URL (Model Context`. Further invocations from upstream: `IVRE contributors. *IVRE, a network recon framework*.`.
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web] in the OSINT category. Fits when the engagement needs: [![Follow on Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/follow/IvreRocks.svg?logo=twitter)](https://twitter.com/IvreRocks)
[![Follow on Mastodon](https://img.shields.io/mastodon/follow/109349639355238149?domain=https%3A%2F%.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: IVRE (Instrument de veille sur les réseaux extérieurs) or DRUNK (Dynamic Recon of UNKnown networks) is a network recon framework, including tools for passive and active recon. IVRE can use data from:.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need OSINT, use ivre because [![Follow on Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/follow/IvreRocks.svg?logo=twitter)](https://twitter.com/IvreRocks)
[![Follow on Mastodon](https://img.shiel.
When the engagement is in the recon phase, use ivre as a cataloged OSINT tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `IVRE ships an [MCP]($KFIOSA_TARGET_URL (Model Context` as documented upstream for ivre.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `IVRE contributors. *IVRE, a network recon framework*.` as documented upstream for ivre.
When chaining OSINT tools, pair ivre with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ivre --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: IVRE contributors. *IVRE, a network recon framework*.
